title: molinia arundinacea var. litoralis
intro: Molinia arundinacea var. litoralis, also known as sea sweetgrass or coastal molinia, is a type of flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellowish-green leaves and delicate purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of sea sweetgrass include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the relief of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also sometimes used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
